Annual Exam - 2023

Code: 65/1/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 38 questions.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:80

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 38 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five Sections - A, B, C, D and E.
  3. In Section A, Questions no. 1 to 18 are multiple choice questions (MCQs) and questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion-Reason based questions of 1 mark each.
  4. In Section B, Questions no. 21 to 25 are very short answer (VSA) type questions, carrying 2 marks each.
  5. In Section C, Questions no. 26 to 31 are short answer (SA) type questions, carrying 3 marks each.
  6. In Section D, Questions no. 32 to 35 are long answer (LA) type questions carrying 5 marks each.
  7. In Section E, Questions no. 36 to 38 are case study based questions carrying 4 marks each.
  8. There is no overall choice. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions in Section B, 3 questions in Section C, 2 questions in Section D and 2 questions in Section E.
  9. Use of calculators is not allowed.

SECTION A

This section comprises multiple choice questions (MCQs) of 1 mark each.
1.

If {tex}\vec {\text a} + \vec {\text b} = \hat {\text i}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} = {tex}2\hat {\text i} - 2\hat {\text j} + 2\hat {\text k}{/tex}, then {tex}|\vec {\text b}|{/tex} equals:

a. {tex}\sqrt {14}{/tex}
b. 3
c. {tex}\sqrt {12}{/tex}
d. {tex}\sqrt {17}{/tex}
2.

The direction ratios of a line parallel to z-axis are:

a. < 1, 1, 0 >
b. < 1, 1, 1 >
c. < 0, 0, 0 >
d. < 0, 0, 1 >
3.

Ashima can hit a target 2 out of 3 times. She tried to hit the target twice. The probability that she missed the target exactly once is

a. {tex}\frac 23{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac 13{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac 49{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac 19{/tex}
4.

The function f(x) = |x| - x is:

a. continuous but not differentiable at x = 0.
b. continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
c. neither continuous nor differentiable at x = 0.
d. differentiable but not continuous at x = 0.
5.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & 1 \\ -1 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and (3I + 4A) (3I - 4A) = x2I, then the value(s) x is/are:

a. {tex}\pm \sqrt{7}{/tex}
b. 0
c. {tex}\pm 5{/tex}
d. 25
6.

The general solution of the differential equation x dy - (1 + x2) dx = dx is:

a. y = {tex}2 \text x+\frac{\text x^3}{3}+\text C{/tex}
b. y = 2 log x + {tex}\frac{\text x^3}{3}+\text C{/tex}
c. y = {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2}+\text C{/tex}
d. y = 2 log x + {tex}\frac{\text x^2}{2}+\text C{/tex}
7.

If f(x) = a(x - cos x) is strictly decreasing in {tex}\mathbb{R}{/tex}, then a belongs to

a. {0}
b. (0, {tex}\infty{/tex})
c. ({tex}-\infty{/tex}, 0)
d. {tex}(-\infty, \infty){/tex}
8.

The corner points of the feasible region in the graphical representation of a linear programming problem are (2, 72), (15, 20) and (40, 15). If z = 18x + 9y be the objective function, then:

a. z is maximum at (2, 72), minimum at (15, 20)
b. z is maximum at (15, 20), minimum at (40, 15)
c. z is maximum at (40, 15), minimum at (15, 20)
d. z is maximum at (40, 15), minimum at (2, 72)
9.

The number of corner points of the feasible region determined by the constraints x - y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, 2y {tex}\leq{/tex} x + 2, x {tex}\geq{/tex} 0, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0 is:

a. 2
b. 3
c. 4
d. 5
10.

If for a square matrix A, A2 - 3A + I = O and A-1 = xA + yI, then the value of x + y is:

a. -2
b. 2
c. 3
d. -3
11.

If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \mathrm{x} & 2 \\ 3 & \mathrm{x}-1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} is a singular matrix, then the product of all possible values of x is:

a. 6
b. -6
c. 0
d. -7
12.

Let A be a 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 matrix such that |adj A| = 64. Then |A| is equal to:

a. 8 only
b. -8 only
c. 64
d. 8 or -8
13.

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 4 \\ 5 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and 2A + B is a null matrix, then B is equal to:

a. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 6 & 8 \\ 10 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
b. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} -6 & -8 \\ -10 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
c. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} 5 & 8 \\ 10 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
d. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} -5 & -8 \\ -10 & -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
14.

The primitive of {tex}\frac{2}{1+\cos 2 \text x}{/tex} is

a. sec2 x
b. 2 sec2 x tan x
c. tan x
d. -cot x
15.

{tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{6}}{/tex}sec2(x - {tex}\frac \pi 6{/tex}) dx is equal to:

a. {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
b. {tex}-\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
c. {tex}\sqrt 3{/tex}
d. {tex}-\sqrt 3{/tex}
16.

The sum of the order and the degree of the differential equate {tex}\frac{\text d^2 \text y}{\text {dx}^2}+\left(\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}\right)^3{/tex} = sin y is:

a. 5
b. 2
c. 3
d. 4
17.

The value of p for which the vectors {tex}2\hat {\text i}{/tex} + {tex}\text p\hat {\text j}{/tex} + {tex}\hat {\text k}{/tex} and {tex}-4\hat {\text i}{/tex} {tex}-6\hat {\text j}{/tex} + {tex}26\hat {\text k}{/tex} are perpendicular to each other, is:

a. 3
b. -3
c. {tex}-\frac {17}{3}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac {17}{3}{/tex}
18.

For what value of {tex}\lambda{/tex}, the projection of vector {tex}\hat {\text i} + \lambda \hat {\text j}{/tex} on vector {tex}\hat {\text i} -\hat {\text j}{/tex} is {tex}\sqrt 2{/tex}?

a. -1
b. 1
c. 0
d. 3

Questions number 19 and 20 are Assertion and Reason based questions carrying 1 mark each. Two statements are given, one labelled Assertion (A) and the other labelled Reason (R). Select the correct answer from the codes (a), (b), (c) and (d) as given below.
19.

Assertion (A): The range of the function f(x) = 2 sin-1 x + {tex}\frac {3\pi}{2}{/tex}, where x {tex}\in{/tex}[-1, 1], is {tex}\left[\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{5 \pi}{2}\right]{/tex}.
Reason (R): The range of the principal value branch of sin-1 (x) is [0, {tex}\pi{/tex}].

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
20.

Assertion (A): Equation of a line passing through the points (1, 2, 3) and (3, -1, 3) is {tex}\frac{\text x-3}{2}=\frac{\text y+1}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text z-3}{0}{/tex}.
Reason (R): Equation of a line passing through points (x1, y1, z1), (x2, y2, z2) is given by {tex}\frac{\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}_1}{\mathrm{x}_2-\mathrm{x}_1}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-\mathrm{y}_1}{\mathrm{y}_2-\mathrm{y}_1}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\mathrm{z}-\mathrm{z}_1}{\mathrm{z}_2-\mathrm{z}_1}{/tex}.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

SECTION B

This section comprises very short answer (VSA) type questions of 2 marks each.
21.

If the product of two positive numbers is 9, find the numbers so that the sum of their squares is minimum.
22.(i)

A function f: A {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} B defined as f(x) = 2x is both one-one and onto. If A = {1, 2, 3, 4}, then find the set B.

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: sin-1 {tex}\left(\sin \frac{3 \pi}{4}\right){/tex} + cos-1{tex}\left(\cos \frac{3 \pi}{4}\right){/tex} + tan-1 (1)
23.

Find all the vectors of magnitude {tex}3\sqrt 3{/tex} which are collinear to vector {tex}\hat {\text i} + \hat {\text j} + \hat {\text k}{/tex}.
24.(i)

Position vectors of the points A, B and C as shown in the figure below are {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex}, {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}, and {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} respectively.

If {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AC}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac 54{/tex} {tex}\overrightarrow {\text {AB}}{/tex}, express {tex}\vec {\text c}{/tex} in terms of {tex}\vec {\text a}{/tex} and {tex}\vec {\text b}{/tex}.

OR
(ii)

Check whether the lines given by equations x = {tex}2\lambda{/tex} + 2, y = {tex}7\lambda{/tex} + 1, z = {tex}-3\lambda{/tex} - 3 and x = {tex}-\mu{/tex} - 2, y = {tex}2\mu{/tex} + 8, z = {tex}4\mu{/tex} + 5 are perpendicular to each other or not.
25.

If x = {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^{\tan ^{-1} \mathrm{t}}}{/tex}, y = {tex}\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^{\cot ^{-1} \mathrm{t}}}{/tex}, then show that {tex}\text x \frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}+\text y{/tex} = 0.

SECTION C

This section comprises short answer (SA) type questions of 3 marks each.
26.(i)

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex} ex sin x dx

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{1}{\cos (\text x-\text a) \cos (\text x-\text b)} \text {dx}{/tex}
27.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}}{/tex}[log (sin x) - log (2 cos x)] dx.
28.

Find: {tex}\int \frac {\text x ^ { 2 } } { \left(\text x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left(\text x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } \text {dx}.{/tex}
29.(i)

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}- \frac {2\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = sin{tex}\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex}.

OR
(ii)

Find the particular solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac {\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}{/tex} = sin(x + y) + sin(x - y), given that when x = {tex}\frac {\pi}{4}{/tex}, y = 0.
30.

Solve the following linear programming problem graphically:
Maximize z = 600x + 400y
subject to the constraints:
x + 2y {tex}\leq{/tex} 12,
2x + y {tex}\leq{/tex} 12,
x + 1.25y {tex}\geq{/tex} 5,
x, y {tex}\geq{/tex} 0
31.(i)

The probability distribution of a random variable X is given below:

X 1 2 3
P(X) {tex}\frac {\text k}{2}{/tex} {tex}\frac {\text k}{3}{/tex} {tex}\frac {\text k}{6}{/tex}
  1. Find the value of k.
  2. Find P(1 {tex}\leq{/tex} X < 3).
  3. Find E(X), the mean of X.

OR
(ii)

A and B are independent events such that P(A {tex}\cap{/tex} {tex}\bar {\text B}{/tex}) = {tex}\frac 14{/tex} and P({tex}\bar {\text A}{/tex} {tex}\cap{/tex} B) = {tex}\frac 16{/tex}. Find P(A) and P(B).

SECTION D

This section comprises long answer (LA) type questions of 5 marks each.
32.(i)

If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find (AB)-1.

OR
(ii)

Solve the following system of equations by matrix method:
x + 2y + 3z = 6
2x - y + z = 2
3x + 2y - 2z = 3
33.(i)

Find the vector and the Cartesian equations of a line passing through the point (1, 2, -4) and parallel to the line joining the points A(3, 3, -5) and B(1, 0, -11). Hence, find the distance between the two lines.

OR
(ii)

Find the equations of the line passing through the points A(1, 2, 3) and B(3, 5, 9). Hence, find the coordinates of the points on this line which are at a distance of 14 units from point B.
34.

Find the area of the region bounded by the lines y = 4x + 5, x + y = 5 and 4y = x + 5, using integration.
35.

A relation R is defined on a set of real numbers {tex}\mathbb R{/tex} as
R = {(x, y): x{tex}\cdot{/tex}y is an irrational number}.
Check whether R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive or not.

SECTION E

This section comprises 3 case study based questions of 4 marks each.

Case Study-1
36.

A tank, as shown in the figure below, formed using a combination of a cylinder and a cone, offers better drainage as compared to a flat bottomed tank.

A tap is connected to such a tank whose conical part is full of water. Water is dripping out from a tap at the bottom at the uniform rate of 2 cm3/s. The semi-vertical angle of the conical tank is 45o.

On the basis of given information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find the volume of water in the tank in terms of its radius r.

 1
(ii)

Find rate of change of radius at an instant when r = {tex}2\sqrt 2{/tex} cm.

 1
(iii)(a)

Find the rate at which the wet surface of the conical tank is decreasing at an instant when radius r = {tex}2\sqrt 2{/tex} cm.

 2

OR
(iii)(b)

Find the rate of change of height h at an instant when slant height is 4 cm.

 2

Case Study-2
37.

There are different types of Yoga which involve the usage of different poses of Yoga Asanas, Meditation and Pranayam as shown in the figure below:

The Venn diagram below represents the probabilities of three different types of Yoga, A, B and C performed by the people of a society. Further, it is given that probability of a member performing type C Yoga is 0.44.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

Find the value of x.

 1
(ii)

Find the value of y.

 1
(iii)(a)

Find {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\frac{\mathrm{C}}{\mathrm{B}}\right){/tex}.

 2

OR
(iii)(b)

Find the probability that a randomly selected person of the society does Yoga of type A or B but not C.

 2

Case Study-3
38.

The equation of the path traced by a roller-coaster is given by the polynomial f(x) = a(x + 9) (x + 1) (x - 3). If the roller-coaster crosses y-axis at a point (0, 1), answer the following:
(i)

Find the value of a.

 2
(ii)

Find f"(x) at x = 1.

 2

The function f(x) = |x| - x is:

b. continuous and differentiable at x = 0.
c. neither continuous nor differentiable at x = 0.
d. differentiable but not continuous at x = 0.
Correct Answer: (a) continuous but not differentiable at x = 0.
Explanation:

Continuous but not differentiable at x = 0.
f(x) = |x| - x
|x| is a continuous function at x=0
x is a continuous function at x=0
{tex}\because{/tex} f(x) is continuous function at x=0

{tex}\ f(x) = \begin{cases} x -x & \quad \text{if } \text{x }\geq0\\ -x-x & \quad \text{if } \text{ x}<0 \end{cases} {/tex}

{tex}\ f(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & \quad \text{if } \text{x }\geq0\\ -2x & \quad \text{if } \text{ x}<0 \end{cases} {/tex}

Therefore, at x=0 f(x) is continuous. However, f(x) is not differentiable at x=0 because the left and right derivatives are not equal.

Specifically, the left derivative is -2 and the right derivative is O, so the function is not differentiable at x=0

Explanation:

({tex}-\infty{/tex}, 0)
We have

Strictly decreasing in R i.e. f'(x) <0

We know that 
-1 {tex}\le{/tex} sin x{tex}\le{/tex}1
-1+1 {tex}\le{/tex} 1+sin x{tex}\le{/tex}1+1
0 {tex}\le{/tex} 1+sin x{tex}\le{/tex}2
0.a {tex}\le{/tex} a(1+sin x){tex}\le{/tex}2a
0 {tex}\le{/tex} a(1+sin x){tex}\le{/tex}2a
Since 1+sin x is always greater and equal to 0. so a must be negative i.e. a<0

let the given integral be,

{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)} \int \frac{\sin (a-b)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)} d x{/tex}

{tex}=\frac{1}{\sin (a-b)}{/tex} {tex}\int\left[\frac{\sin (x-b) \cdot \cos (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}-\frac{\cos (x-b) \cdot \sin (x-a)}{\cos (x-a) \cdot \cos (x-b)}\right] d x{/tex}

We get, I = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac {\pi}{2}}{/tex}log {tex}\left(\frac{\cot \mathrm{x}}{2}\right){/tex}dx

28.

Find: {tex}\int \frac {\text x ^ { 2 } } { \left(\text x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left(\text x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } \text {dx}.{/tex}

Solution:

Given, I = {tex}\int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
Consider, {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) }{/tex}
Put, {tex}x^2 = t{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } = \frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
Using partial fractions, 
 {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { A } { ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { B } { ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow  t = A(t + 9) + B(t + 4){/tex}
Putting t = -9, 
{tex}\Rightarrow-9 = -5B{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad B = \frac { 9 } { 5 }{/tex}
Putting t = -4, 
{tex}\Rightarrow - 4 = 5 A {/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow A = \frac { - 4 } { 5 }{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\frac { t } { ( t + 4 ) ( t + 9 ) } = \frac { - 4 } { 5 ( t + 4 ) } + \frac { 9 } { 5 ( t + 9 ) }{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad I = \int \frac { x ^ { 2 } } { \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \int \frac { - 4 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 4 \right) } d x + \int \frac { 9 } { 5 \left( x ^ { 2 } + 9 \right) } d x{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 2 ) ^ { 2 } } + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \int \frac { d x } { x ^ { 2 } + ( 3 ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { - 4 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 2 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + \frac { 9 } { 5 } \cdot \frac { 1 } { 3 } \cdot \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) + C{/tex}
{tex}\left[ \because \int \frac { 1 } { x ^ { 2 } + a ^ { 2 } } d x = \frac { 1 } { a } \tan ^ { - 1 } \frac { x } { a } + C \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 3 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 3 } \right) - \frac { 2 } { 5 } \tan ^ { - 1 } \left( \frac { x } { 2 } \right) + C{/tex}

Find the general solution of the differential equation: {tex}\frac{\text {dy}}{\text {dx}}- \frac {2\text y}{\text x}{/tex} = sin{tex}\frac {1}{\text x}{/tex}.

Integrating factor = {tex}\text e^{\int-\frac{2}{\text x} \text {dx}}{/tex} = e-2 log x = {tex}\frac{1}{\text x^2}{/tex}
Solution is {tex}\mathrm{y} \cdot \frac{1}{\mathrm{x}^2}{/tex} = {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}dx + C

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\int \frac{1}{\text x^2} \cdot \sin \frac{1}{\text x}{/tex}dx = {tex}-\int{/tex}sin t dt = cos t = cos {tex}\frac{1}{\mathrm{x}}{/tex}

Find the vector and the Cartesian equations of a line passing through the point (1, 2, -4) and parallel to the line joining the points A(3, 3, -5) and B(1, 0, -11). Hence, find the distance between the two lines.

Solution:

Vector equation of required line through (1, 2, -4) is
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\text i}+2 \hat{\text j}-4 \hat{\text k}{/tex} + {tex}\lambda(2 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}){/tex}
and cartesian equation: {tex}\frac{\text x-1}{2}=\frac{\text y-2}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\text z+4}{6}{/tex}
Equation of line through A(3, 3, -5) and B(1, 0, -11) is
{tex}\vec{r}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}-5 \hat{\text k}{/tex} + {tex}\mu(2 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}){/tex}
Distance between parallel lines is given by d = {tex}\frac{\left|\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\vec{a}_1\right) \times \vec{b}\right|}{|\vec{b}|}{/tex}
Here {tex}\vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}+6 \hat{\text k}{/tex}, {tex}\vec{a}_1{/tex} = {tex}\hat{\text i}+2 \hat{\text j}-4 \hat{\text k}{/tex}, {tex}\overrightarrow{a_2}{/tex} = {tex}3 \hat{\text i}+3 \hat{\text j}-5 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\vec{a}_1\right){/tex} = {tex}2 \hat{\text i}+\hat{\text j}-\hat{\text k}{/tex}
{tex}\left(\overrightarrow{a_2}-\overrightarrow{a_1}\right) \times \vec{b}{/tex} = {tex}9 \hat{\text i}-14 \hat{\text j}+4 \hat{\text k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} d = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{293}}{7}{/tex}

35.

A relation R is defined on a set of real numbers {tex}\mathbb R{/tex} as
R = {(x, y): x{tex}\cdot{/tex}y is an irrational number}.
Check whether R is reflexive, symmetric and transitive or not.

Solution:

For each value x.y {tex}\in{/tex} R, x-y is an irrational number.
(1) For Reflexive
as x-x=0 is a rational number
so, (x,x) {tex}\notin{/tex} R as 0 is not irrational.
R is not reflexive
(2) For symmetric
Let (x, y) {tex}\in{/tex} R
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x-y is an irrational number
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (y-x) is an irrational number
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (y, x) {tex}\in{/tex} R
{tex}\therefore{/tex} R is symmetric
(3) For Transitive
(1, {tex}\sqrt 2{/tex}) {tex}\in{/tex} R, ({tex}\sqrt 2{/tex}, 2) {tex}\in{/tex} R (or any other counter-example)
but (1, 2) {tex}\notin{/tex} R
{tex}\therefore{/tex} R is not transitive
